Save Report this job Project Role : Web Developer Project Role Description : Design, build and test web-based applications for various site components and edit site content. Document technical designs and specifications. Research and incorporate updated content for websites. Must have skills : Sitecore Good to have skills : Microsoft Front End Development (FED) NextJS, Sitecore MVC, React.js Minimum 5 Year(s) Of Experience Is Required Educational Qualification : 15 years full time education Summary: As a Web Developer, a typical day involves designing, constructing, and thoroughly testing web-based applications that support various components of a website. The role includes editing and updating site content to ensure accuracy and relevance. Additionally, the position requires documenting technical designs and specifications in detail. Continuous research is conducted to integrate the latest content and improvements into websites, ensuring they remain current and engaging for users. Collaboration with different teams to align web functionalities with project goals is also a key part of the daily routine. Roles & Responsibilities: Lead end-to-end design and development of headless solutions using Sitecore XM Cloud Architect scalable frontend applications using Next.js and React Define coding standards, architecture guidelines, and best practices Perform code reviews and ensure high-quality deliverables Build and integrate APIs, microservices, and third-party systems Implement personalization, analytics, and content delivery strategies in Sitecore Optimize applications for performance, SEO, and accessibility Ensure CI/CD pipelines and DevOps best practices are followed Professional & Technical Skills: Must To Have Skills: Proficiency in Sitecore XM Cloud / Headless CMS architecture, Next.js, React, and modern JavaScript (ES6+) Experience with Sitecore JSS (JavaScript Services) Strong understanding of REST APIs / GraphQL Should Have REST API integration Experience with Headless content delivery architectures Should Have CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, etc.) Should Have Containerization (Docker preferred) Good to have experience with Sitecore Personalize, CDP, or Experience Edge Good to Have Knowledge of microfrontend architecture Good to have Familiarity with edge functions and CDN strategies Good to Have Experience with Azure / AWS Cloud platforms Additional Information: The candidate should have minimum 5 years of experience in Sitecore. This position is based at our Pune office. A 15 years full time education is required. jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Mechatronics) (M/F/D)*
jobs.description: Senior Software Engineer (Mechatronics) (M/F/D)* TGW Logistics Pune District, Maharashtra, India 20 hours ago Be among the first 25 applicants See who TGW Logistics has hired for this role Apply Join or sign in to find your next job Join to apply for the Senior Software Engineer (Mechatronics) (M/F/D)* role at TGW Logistics Email or phone Password Show Forgot password? Sign in Sign in with Email or New to LinkedIn? Join now By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn’s User Agreement , Privacy Policy , and Cookie Policy . Save Report this job We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Global Software Engineering & Technology team in Pune, India. In this role, you will design, develop, test, and enhance high-quality software components within the TGW architecture for global warehouse automation solutions.You will work on complex technical modules, contribute to software architecture, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and support the development of scalable, reliable, and high-performance applications. What you'll be handling: Design, develop, and maintain backend applications using .NET technologies. Develop responsive frontend applications using Angular (preferred) or React. Build and integrate RESTful APIs and backend services. Develop software for controlling and monitoring TGW mechatronic systems. Write clean, maintainable, and testable code following engineering best practices. Participate in code reviews and mentor junior developers. Collaborate with Product Owners, QA, Controls Engineering, and global development teams throughout the SDLC. Perform system integration, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis. Contribute to unit, integration, and system testing to ensure software quality and reliability. Support CI/CD, deployment automation, and continuous improvement initiatives. What you'll need: Required Skills 4–8 years of experience in .NET Full Stack development. Strong experience with .NET/.NET Core, ASP.NET Core, C#, and Web APIs. Hands-on experience with Angular (preferred) or React, HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript/TypeScript. Good understanding of REST APIs, Microservices, and distributed systems. Strong knowledge of SQL databases; exposure to NoSQL is an advantage. Good understanding of OOP, SOLID Principles, Design Patterns, and Clean Code. Experience with Git, CI/CD pipelines, Docker, and Kubernetes. Experience working in Agile (Scrum/Kanban) environments. Good to Have Experience with Redis, RabbitMQ, or MQTT. Exposure to Azure or AWS cloud platforms. Knowledge of Domain-Driven Design (DDD). Experience in automation, warehouse management, or industrial software. Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or a related field. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. What you'll receive: Opportunity to work with a global leader in warehouse automation, robotics, and logistics solutions. Training and development programs, including internal learning opportunities and career growth support. Modern work environment with advanced technology and collaborative teams. Flexible working arrangements (availability varies by role and location). International career opportunities across Europe, Asia, and North America. *TGW is an employer that promotes equal opportunities. We invite everybody interested in this job to apply. jobs.description: Senior Software Engineer - Digital Twin & React Web Development Caterpillar Inc. Bengaluru East, Karnataka, India 15 hours ago 74 applicants See who Caterpillar Inc. has hired for this role Apply Join or sign in to find your next job Join to apply for the Senior Software Engineer - Digital Twin & React Web Development role at Caterpillar Inc. Email or phone Password Show Forgot password? Sign in Sign in with Email or New to LinkedIn? Join now By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn’s User Agreement , Privacy Policy , and Cookie Policy . Save Report this job Career Area Technology, Digital and Data Job Description Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc. When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it. JD: Python & React Web Engineer (Digital Twin) Key Responsibilities Application Development Design, develop, and maintain scalable web applications using Python (backend) and ReactJS (frontend). Pipeline Development Build and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines to support application data workflows and integrations. Data Quality Implement validation, monitoring, and data quality checks to ensure accuracy and consistency across systems. Automation Automate data ingestion, processing, and application workflows for improved efficiency and reliability. Collaboration Partner with data engineers, frontend developers, designers, and product teams to build cohesive solutions. Infrastructure Support Work with cloud or on-prem environments to ensure application availability, scalability, and performance. Monitoring & Troubleshooting Monitor application performance and data pipelines, diagnose issues, and resolve them proactively. Required Skills Strong Python programming skills (FastAPI, Flask, or Django). Solid experience with ReactJS and modern frontend development (hooks, state management, REST APIs). Experience with data platforms such as Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift. Hands-on experience with workflow tools like Airflow, Prefect, or Luigi. Understanding of data governance and data quality practices. Experience building and integrating RESTful APIs. Good to Have Basic understanding of Digital Twin concepts. Exposure to NVIDIA Omniverse or similar simulation/3D platforms. Familiarity with real-time data processing (Kafka, streaming pipelines). Experience working with 3D data, IoT, or sensor-driven systems. Knowledge of USD (Universal Scene Description) or visualization pipelines. This position requires working onsite five days a week. Visa Sponsorship is not available for this position. Posting Dates July 13, 2026 - July 23, 2026 Caterpillar is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants of any age are encouraged to apply Not ready to apply? Join our Talent Community.
